title: Add predefined rotating button text constants for Welcome screen
body:
## Summary
Create a constant array of strings to be used as rotating labels on the Welcome screen's primary CTA button.

## Context
The Welcome screen (`apps/launcher/src/pages/WelcomeScreen.tsx`) currently has a hard-coded button label `"How do errors happen?"`. We want to rotate through multiple labels with animation. This issue covers defining the text constants.

## Requirements
- Create a constant array (e.g. `ROTATING_BUTTON_TEXTS`) either at the top of `WelcomeScreen.tsx` or in a shared config file:
```ts
const ROTATING_BUTTON_TEXTS = [
  "How do errors happen?",
  "See AI detect objects",
  "Try speech transcription",
  "Explore agentic workflows"
];
```
- The first string should match the current hard-coded label
- Export the constant if placed in a separate config file so it can be tested independently

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Constant array is defined and accessible to the WelcomeScreen component
- [ ] Array contains the 4 specified strings in order
- [ ] Existing button behavior is unchanged

title: Implement text rotation logic with useState/useEffect for Welcome screen button
body:
## Summary
Add React state management and interval-based rotation logic to cycle through the predefined button texts on the Welcome screen.

## Context
Depends on the predefined text constants being available (see: "Add predefined rotating button text constants for Welcome screen"). The Welcome screen button (`apps/launcher/src/pages/WelcomeScreen.tsx`, class `welcome-cta`) needs to cycle through an array of label strings on a timed interval.

## Requirements
- Add `useState` for:
  - `activeIndex` (number) — tracks which string in the array is currently displayed
  - `isFading` (boolean) — controls whether the text is in a fade-out state
- Add `useEffect` with `setInterval` to schedule transitions:
  - Every ~4000ms (configurable via a constant like `ROTATION_INTERVAL_MS = 4000`):
    1. Set `isFading` to `true` (triggers fade-out)
    2. After fade-out duration (~300ms), update `activeIndex` to next string (wrapping around)
    3. Set `isFading` to `false` (triggers fade-in)
  - Use `setTimeout` inside the interval callback for the mid-transition swap
- Properly clean up both `setInterval` and any pending `setTimeout` on component unmount to avoid memory leaks
- Avoid re-creating intervals unnecessarily; keep hook dependencies stable
- Render the button text from the array using `activeIndex`: `ROTATING_BUTTON_TEXTS[activeIndex]`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Button text automatically cycles through all strings in order
- [ ] Cycle loops back to the first string after the last
- [ ] Default interval is 4000ms between transitions
- [ ] All timers are cleaned up on unmount (no console warnings in strict mode)
- [ ] No unnecessary re-renders or interval recreation

title: Add CSS fade animation for Welcome screen rotating button text
body:
## Summary
Add CSS-based opacity transitions to create a smooth fade-out/fade-in effect when the button label text changes.

## Context
The Welcome screen button (`apps/launcher/src/pages/WelcomeScreen.css`, class `welcome-cta`) already has `transition: opacity 0.15s ease` for its `:active` state. We need to extend/adjust the transition to support the text rotation animation driven by the `isFading` state.

## Requirements
- Add a CSS class (e.g., `.welcome-cta-text`) for the `<span>` wrapping the button label text inside the button
- Add CSS transition on opacity for the text span:
  ```css
  .welcome-cta-text {
    transition: opacity 300ms ease;
    opacity: 1;
  }
  .welcome-cta-text.fading {
    opacity: 0;
  }
  ```
- Timings should be:
  - Fade-out: ~250–300ms
  - Text swap happens while opacity is 0
  - Fade-in: ~250–300ms
  - Hold visible for ~3–4 seconds before next cycle
- Keep the existing button styling (black background, white text, border-radius, min-height, tap highlight) intact
- Ensure the button does not visibly resize or jump when text changes (the `min-height: 56px` and fixed `max-width: 360px` should help)
- The button's existing `:active` opacity style should still work alongside the text fade

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Text fades out smoothly before changing
- [ ] Text fades in smoothly after changing
- [ ] Button size remains stable during transitions
- [ ] Existing button hover/active styles still work
- [ ] Animation feels subtle and readable

title: Add accessibility support for Welcome screen rotating button text
body:
## Summary
Ensure the rotating button text is accessible: respect reduced motion preferences, and provide proper screen reader announcements.

## Context
The Welcome screen button in `apps/launcher/src/pages/WelcomeScreen.tsx` will have animated rotating text (fade transitions). We need to ensure this is accessible per WCAG guidelines.

## Requirements

### Reduced motion support
- Use `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` media query to disable the fade animation:
  - Option A (CSS): Under `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)`, set `transition: none` on `.welcome-cta-text`
  - Option B (JS): Use `window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)')` to either skip the fade (instant swap) or keep fixed text
  - Either approach is acceptable; CSS-only is simpler

### Screen reader support
- Add `aria-live="polite"` on the text span so screen readers announce text changes without being disruptive
- Alternatively, use `aria-label` on the button with a static description (e.g., "Explore demos") so screen readers get a stable label while sighted users see the rotating text
- The button must remain keyboard-focusable and activatable (it already is a `<button>`)

### Testing
- Add unit tests that verify:
  - The `prefers-reduced-motion` media query is present in CSS or the JS hook respects it
  - The button has appropriate ARIA attributes
  - Text rotation works correctly (timer-based, can be tested with fake timers)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Users with `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` see no fade animation (instant swap or static text)
- [ ] Screen readers can announce the button's purpose without confusion
- [ ] Button remains keyboard accessible
- [ ] Unit tests cover rotation logic, accessibility attributes, and reduced motion behavior
